title: 10 best herbal decoctions for itchy eyes
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for itchy eyes are a natural and effective remedy to soothe and relieve irritation caused by dryness, allergies, or infections. By infusing herbs in hot water, these decoctions create a gentle and soothing liquid that can be applied directly to the affected area, providing instant relief from itching, burning, and redness. Examples of herbal decoctions that help with itchy eyes include chamomile, calendula, and peppermint, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that calm the eye surface and reduce swelling. For those who suffer from chronic dry eyes or seasonal allergies, these decoctions can greatly improve their quality of life by providing long-lasting relief and allowing them to enjoy everyday activities without discomfort or distraction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower decoctions helps with itchy eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The decoction's active compounds, such as ursolic acid and rosmarinic acid, work to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, while also suppressing the release of histamine, a chemical that can exacerbate itchiness. Additionally, the decoction's soothing properties help to calm irritated conjunctiva and provide fast relief from itchy, burning sensations. As a result, purple coneflower decoctions offer a natural and effective solution for alleviating itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Purple coneflower decoctions contain compounds like triterpenoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and redness in itchy eyes.

Antihistamine: The flavonoids and polyphenols present in purple coneflower decoctions can inhibit the release of histamines, a chemical mediator responsible for allergic reactions, including itchy eyes.

Antioxidant: Purple coneflower decoctions are rich in antioxidants like quercetin, which can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, contributing to dry, itchy eyes by protecting the eyes' delicate tissues from damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming itchy eyes.
Buds: The buds contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and alleviate itchiness associated with eye irritations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried echinacea purpurea flowers or 4-6 teaspoons of fresh flowers from a trusted source.
Rinse the dried flowers with cool water to remove any debris and impurities.
Combine the rinsed flowers with 8 ounces of boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant glass measuring cup.
Steep the mixture for 15-20 minutes, allowing the echinacea to infuse its medicinal properties into the decoction.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold decoctions helps with itchy eyes because its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties soothe and calm irritated eye tissues. The decoction's natural flavonoids and carotenoids work to reduce redness, swelling, and itchiness, providing quick relief from uncomfortable dry or irritated eyes. As the decoction penetrates the eye area, it helps to neutralize any underlying infection or allergen, promoting healthy tear production and leaving eyes feeling refreshed and comfortable.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold's decoction has anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with itchy eyes.
Antimicrobial properties: The decoction's antimicrobial agents help combat bacterial, viral, and fungal infections that can cause itchy eyes, promoting a healthy eye environment.

Anti-allergic properties: Pot marigold's decoction contains compounds that neutralize histamine, a chemical released during allergic reactions, alleviating itching, redness, and irritation in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are valued for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them effective in reducing itchiness and discomfort in the eyes.
Barks: The barks have been traditionally used to treat eye irritations and allergies due to its astringent and anti-pruritic effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers and dry them thoroughly in a warm place for at least two hours.
Combine one teaspoon of dried calendula petals with one cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 15-20 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse their medicinal properties into the liquid.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Refrigerate the decoction for up to three days before using it as an eyewash to soothe and calm itchy eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright decoctions helps with itchy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The herb, Euphrasia officinalis, contains bioactive compounds that soothe and calm irritated eye tissues, reducing itching and inflammation. Additionally, eyebright decoctions help to loosen and flush out excess mucus and debris from the eye, further relieving itchiness and discomfort. This natural remedy provides a gentle yet effective solution for soothing itchy eyes and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Eyebright's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and itching in the eyes, providing relief from irritation and discomfort.

Antihistamine-like action: The active compounds in eyebright, such as flavonoids and terpenes, have an antihistamine-like effect, which helps to reduce itchiness, redness, and congestion in the eyes caused by allergic reactions or irritants.

Expectorant property: Eyebright's expectorant properties help to loosen and remove excess mucus and other debris from the eyes, further reducing itchiness and discomfort by clearing out irritating substances.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain a high concentration of alkaloids, which help to reduce inflammation and itching in the eyes.
Flowers: Eyebright flowers are used due to their ability to soothe and calm irritated eye tissue, providing relief from itchiness and redness.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried euphrasia officinalis leaves.
Add the measured euphrasia leaves to one cup of boiling distilled water.
Reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes to extract active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Allow the decoction to cool before storing it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Watercress decoctions helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The decoction's natural compounds, such as flavonoids and vitamin A, work to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, providing instant relief from itchiness and discomfort. Additionally, watercress's antibacterial and antiviral properties help combat infections that can cause itchy eyes, promoting healthy tear production and reducing inflammation, leaving your eyes feeling calm, cool, and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Watercress contains high amounts of antioxidants, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, thus alleviating itchiness and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in watercress decoction, such as flavonoids and saponins, can help to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from itchy eyes.

Antimicrobial property: Watercress has antimicrobial properties that can help to combat bacterial or fungal infections that may cause itchy eyes, promoting a healthy eye environment and reducing discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Watercress roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with itchy eyes.
Barks: The barks of the watercress plant contain antioxidants and flavonoids that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh nasturtium leaves and flowers for use in the decoction.
Combine chopped nasturtium with boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean pot.
Cool the decoction to room temperature before using it as an eye treatment.
Gently rinse your eyes with the cooled decoction several times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe decoctions helps with itchy eyes because its anti-inflammatory properties soothe irritated eyelids and conjunctiva. The gentle, non-irritating liquid calms redness and reduces itchiness caused by allergens, dry air, or other environmental factors. Aloe's antimicrobial agents also combat bacterial and viral infections that may contribute to itchy eyes. Additionally, the decoction's moisturizing properties help lubricate dry eyes, providing long-lasting relief from discomfort and itchiness. Overall, aloe decoctions provide natural, effective relief for itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from itching and discomfort.
Antioxidant: Aloe's antioxidants neutralize free radicals in the eyes, protecting them from damage caused by oxidative stress and reducing inflammation that can lead to itchiness.
Soothing and moisturizing: The mucilages present in aloe help soothe and moisturize the eyes, reducing dryness, irritation, and itching caused by conditions like conjunctivitis or blepharitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the aloe plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing itchy eyes and reducing redness.
Leaves: Aloe leaves contain gel-like substance with antioxidants and vitamins that help calm and moisturize itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera gel from mature leaves for its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties.
Measure 20g of dried aloe vera powder or 50ml of pure aloe vera gel and grind into fine paste.
Combine the ground aloe vera with 500ml of distilled water in a saucepan and bring to boil then reduce heat.
Simmer the mixture for 10 minutes to allow the active ingredients to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the decoction through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store in refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender decoctions helps with itchy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The decoction's soothing and calming effects can reduce redness, puffiness, and irritation caused by allergies or dryness. As a gentle and non-irritating solution, English lavender decoction can effectively alleviate itchiness, scratchiness, and discomfort, providing relief for itchy eyes. Its subtle aroma also promotes relaxation and reduces stress, further contributing to its beneficial effects on eye health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to itchy eyes by alleviating irritation and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help to soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and itchiness caused by allergic reactions or other inflammatory responses.

Antihistamine properties: Lavender's antihistamine effects can help to counteract the release of histamine, a chemical released in response to allergens, which can exacerbate itchy eyes by causing itching, burning, and redness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes, providing relief from itchiness.
Seeds: The seeds contain saponins, which have antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial or fungal infections causing eye itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and add them to a heat-resistant glass measuring cup.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the lavandula flowers, covering them completely, and let steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Store the prepared decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days, using it as needed by adding 1-2 teaspoons to each eye bath water.
To use, rinse eyes with lukewarm water, then add the lavender decoction to a bowl of warm water and bathe the eyes gently for 5 minutes twice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm decoctions helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated ocular tissues. The herb's antiseptic compounds also help to reduce redness and discomfort by combating underlying infections or allergies that may be causing the itchiness. Additionally, lemon balm's cooling and calming effects can provide instant relief from dry, itchy eyes, making it a natural and effective remedy for soothing irritated peepers.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, help reduce redness and inflammation in the eyes, alleviating itchiness.

Anti-allergic and antihistamine effects: The flavonoids present in lemon balm, particularly quercetin, have been shown to inhibit allergic reactions and block histamine release, which can contribute to itchy eyes.
Sedative and calming properties: Lemon balm's sedative and calming effects can help relax the eye muscles and reduce irritation, making it an effective remedy for soothing itchy eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Leaves: Known for their antiseptic and antibacterial properties, leaves are effective in reducing redness and inflammation associated with itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Rinse 2 tablespoons of dried melissa officinalis leaves in cold water.
Combine the rinsed herb with 8 ounces of boiling water in a saucepan.
Steep the mixture for 10 minutes allowing the herbs to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass cup.
Let the cooled decoction stand at room temperature for 1 hour before refrigerating.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with itchy eyes because its unique compounds, such as aristolochic acid and other bioactive agents, have a natural anti-inflammatory effect that soothes and calms irritated eye tissues. The decoction's antihistamine properties also help to reduce itchiness and swelling associated with allergic reactions or conjunctivitis. Additionally, the gentle, non-irritating nature of the decoction makes it an effective remedy for soothing dry, itchy eyes without causing further discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Maidenhair tree decoctions contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eye, which can contribute to itchy eyes.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds can help alleviate itchiness by reducing swelling and redness in the eyes.
Wetting and lubricating properties: The mucilages present in maidenhair tree decoctions can help soothe and moisturize dry, irritated eyes, providing relief from itchiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Buds: The buds contain anti-inflammatory properties that alleviate redness and discomfort associated with itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Rinse 10 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with distilled water and drain thoroughly.
Combine the rinsed ginkgo biloba leaves with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.
Store the cooled decoction in the refrigerator for up to 7 days and use as needed by dropping one to two drops into each affected eye.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Candelilla decoctions helps with itchy eyes because they provide a soothing and calming effect on the conjunctiva, reducing inflammation and irritation. The anti-inflammatory properties of candelilla help to alleviate itchiness and discomfort caused by dryness, allergies, or other irritants. Additionally, candelilla's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial infections that can exacerbate itchy eyes, making it an effective natural remedy for relief from itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Candelilla decoctions contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and itching in the eyes, providing relief from redness and irritation.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of candelilla help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to itchy and irritated eyes.
Soften and lubricate: Candelilla decoctions have a soothing and emollient effect on the mucous membranes in the eyes, helping to soften and lubricate them, thereby reducing itchiness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Candelilla roots are used to make a soothing decoction that calms irritated and itchy eyes by reducing inflammation.
Barks: The barks of the candelilla plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate redness, swelling, and itchiness in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality euphorbia officinarum roots weighing approximately 250-300 grams for this recipe.
Clean and chop the harvested roots into small pieces to ensure maximum extractability.
Combine one part of chopped euphorbia officinarum roots with eight parts of distilled water in a large saucepan.
Simmer the mixture over medium heat for 20-25 minutes or until the decoction reduces to approximately half its original volume.
Strain the decoction through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and store it in the refrigerator for up to five days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in chamomile flowers work to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in the eyes. When used as a warm compress or rinse, chamomile decoction can help soothe dry, itchy eyes and calm allergic reactions. Additionally, its gentle, non-irritating nature makes it an excellent natural remedy for sensitive eyes, providing relief from discomfort and itchiness without any harsh chemicals or artificial additives.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness.
Antihistamine effect: Chamomile's flavonoids and terpenoids have been shown to inhibit histamine release, which helps alleviate itchy eyes caused by allergic reactions or other irritants.
Soothing and calming effects: Chamomile's apigenin and other flavonoids work as natural sedatives, relaxing the eye muscles and reducing itchiness by promoting a sense of calm and reducing stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant contain a high concentration of apigenin, an antioxidant compound that helps soothe and calm irritated eye tissues.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, providing quick relief from itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning after dew has dried for optimal potency.
Rinse one tablespoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers or two tablespoons of fresh flowers with 250 milliliters of cold filtered water.
Combine rinsed herbs and cold water in a saucepan and heat slowly over low heat for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding solids after 10 minutes.
Allow the decoction to cool at room temperature for 30 minutes before refrigerating it in an airtight container for up to three days use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eye tissue. The decoction's high concentration of antioxidants also helps to reduce oxidative stress, a common underlying cause of itchiness in the eyes. Additionally, dandelion's natural astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling, providing quick relief from itching and discomfort. By using dandelion decoctions as a gentle and natural remedy, individuals can find effective relief for itchy eyes without harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in dandelion decoction can neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress that may contribute to eye irritation and itchiness, promoting overall eye health.
Antibacterial: Dandelion's antibacterial properties can help combat bacterial infections or allergies that may be causing itchy eyes, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy eyes.
Flowers: Dandelion flowers contain flavonoids, which have natural antihistamine effects that soothe itchiness and itching caused by allergies.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 10-15 grams of dried taraxacum officinale root material.
Boil one liter of distilled water in a pot and add the weighed root material.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let steep for 20-25 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container.
Store the cooled decoction in an airtight container for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort decoctions helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The decoction, made from the dried flowers of the plant, can reduce redness and swelling in the eyes caused by allergies or irritants. As a natural demulcent, St John's Wort soothes and calms the eye tissues, providing fast relief from itching, burning, and grittiness. Its ability to reduce histamine release also helps to alleviate itchy eyes triggered by allergic reactions, making it a popular natural remedy for irritated peepers.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine and anti-inflammatory effects: St. John's Wort contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that can help reduce histamine release and inflammation in the eye, providing relief from itchiness.
Antioxidant properties: The plant's antioxidant compounds can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to itchy eyes.

Soothing and anti-allergic effects: St. John's Wort's decoction has been traditionally used as a wash or rinse for soothing irritated eyes, reducing redness, and calming allergic reactions that cause itchiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in soothing itchy eyes.
Flowers: The flowers are rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that can help reduce inflammation and itching in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Rinse dried st john's wort flowers and leaves with filtered water to remove impurities.
Measure one tablespoon of dried hypericum perforatum material per cup of distilled water.
Combine the measured ingredients in a medium-sized pot and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes, or until the liquid has reduced by half.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain decoctions helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated ocular tissues. The decoction's natural antiseptic qualities also help to reduce redness and swelling, providing fast relief from itching and discomfort. Additionally, plantain has been shown to have a gentle yet effective ability to loosen and remove impurities from the eye surface, further contributing to its alleviating effects on itchy eyes. Overall, plantain decoctions offer a natural and non-invasive way to alleviate itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Plantain decoctions contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing redness and swelling associated with itchy eyes.

Antihistamine property: The allergenic compounds present in plantain decoctions can neutralize histamine, the chemical released during allergic reactions that causes itchiness, tearing, and redness in the eyes.
Soothing and protective property: Plantain's mucilages (gel-like substances) provide a soothing, protective barrier on the ocular surface, calming irritated tissues and preventing further irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Plantain roots are often used in decoctions to reduce inflammation and itching associated with allergic reactions, such as conjunctivitis.
Barks: The barks of the plantain plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe itchy eyes and reduce redness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves and rinse them gently under cold running water.
Combine 30-40 grams of dried or 60-80 grams of fresh plantago major leaves in a large pot.
Boil the mixture in 500 milliliters of distilled water for 5-7 minutes to extract the active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Allow the decoction to cool at room temperature before refrigerating it for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle decoctions helps with itchy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and itching in the eyelids and conjunctiva. The decoction's antihistamine effects also help alleviate itchy eyes caused by allergic reactions or environmental irritants. Additionally, stinging nettle contains antioxidants that soothe and calm irritated tissues, providing long-lasting relief from itchiness and discomfort. As a natural remedy, it can be used in conjunction with other treatments to provide effective and gentle relief for itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Stinging nettle decoctions have been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, which helps to alleviate itchiness and discomfort associated with eye conditions such as conjunctivitis.

Antihistamine property: The decoction's antihistamine effects help to counteract the release of histamines, which are released during allergic reactions and can cause itchy eyes, redness, and swelling.

Soften-and-cleanse property: Stinging nettle's mucilage content helps to soften and remove irritants from the eyes, making it an effective treatment for removing allergens or debris that may be contributing to itchiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the stinging nettle plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eyes, making them effective in relieving itchiness.
Barks: The barks of the stinging nettle plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that reduce inflammation and itching, providing a natural remedy for itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh nettle leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cool running water.
Combine 1 cup of dried nettle leaves or 2 cups of fresh nettle leaves with 2 quarts of distilled water.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Allow the decoction to cool and refrigerate it before using as an eye wash.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian decoctions helps with itchy eyes because its anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated eye tissue. The herb's natural sedative qualities also help to reduce redness and swelling, providing fast relief from dry, itchy, or inflamed eyes. Additionally, valerian's antioxidant properties protect the eyes from further irritation and damage caused by environmental factors, such as pollutants and allergens. By using herbal valerian decoctions, individuals can experience a natural and effective solution for soothing itchy, irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine effect: Valerian's antihistamine properties help to reduce itching, redness, and swelling caused by allergic reactions or irritation in the eyes.
Anti-inflammatory action: Valerian's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with conditions like conjunctivitis or blepharitis.
Calming effect on the nervous system: Valerian's calming properties help to relax the nerves around the eyes, reducing anxiety and stress that can exacerbate eye itching and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are rich in essential oils and sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe itchy eyes.
Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes contain a high concentration of valerenic acid, which has analgesic and antispasmodic effects, relieving eye irritation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots, measure out 2 teaspoons for every cup of water.
Rinse the roots gently with cold running water to remove any debris.
Boil 4 cups of distilled or filtered water in a saucepan over high heat for 5 minutes.
Reduce the heat and add the valeriana roots, simmer for 10-15 minutes to extract the active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregon grape decoctions helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eye tissues. The decoction's antiseptic compounds also help to reduce redness and swelling caused by allergic reactions or other irritants. Additionally, the natural antioxidants in Oregon grape decoctions have been shown to protect the delicate tissue around the eyes from damage, promoting a healthy and comfortable visual experience. By reducing inflammation and soothing irritation, Oregon grape decoctions provide effective relief for itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anthocyanosides: These bioflavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching in the eyes.
Berberine: This alkaloid has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat eye infections that may be contributing to itchiness and inflammation.
Vitexin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help soothe itchy, irritated eyes and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy eyes.
Barks: Contains astringent properties that constrict blood vessels, reducing itchiness and discomfort caused by eye irritation.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality berberis aquifolium roots and leaves measuring about 20-25 grams total.
Rinse the gathered materials under cold running water then chop them into small pieces.
Combine the chopped plant material in a heat-resistant glass container with 500 milliliters of distilled water.
Bring the mixture to a boil on high heat then reduce it to low heat and simmer for 20-25 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow gentian decoctions helps with itchy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated ocular tissues. The decoction's flavonoids and alkaloids work to reduce redness and swelling, while its antibacterial and antiviral compounds help eliminate underlying infections or allergens that may be contributing to itchiness. As a result, yellow gentian decoctions provide effective relief from itchy eyes, restoring comfort and clarity to the visual field.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yellow gentian contains compounds that reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from itching and discomfort caused by allergic reactions or irritants.

Antihistamine-like effects: The decoction has been shown to possess antihistamine-like properties, which help to counteract histamine release and alleviate symptoms such as itching, redness, and watering of the eyes.

Antimicrobial activity: Yellow gentian's antimicrobial properties can help to combat infections that may cause itchy eyes, such as conjunctivitis, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing itchy and irritated eyes.
Barks: The barks of the yellow gentian plant are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and relieve itchiness in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Measure 10 grams of dried gentiana lutea root and add it to a clean glass jar.
Boil 500 milliliters of water in a saucepan for 5-7 minutes to sterilize the equipment.
Combine the gentiana lutea root with the boiling water in a large ceramic pot.
Simmer the mixture at medium heat for 20-30 minutes, then strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Allow the decoction to cool and store it in an amber glass bottle refrigerated at 3-5°c for up to 5 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow decoctions helps with itchy eyes because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated ocular tissues. The mucilages present in marshmallow roots and leaves form a protective barrier on the surface of the eye, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, the decoction's demulcent properties help to lubricate and moisturize the eyes, providing long-lasting relief from itching and dryness. Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, easing discomfort and itchiness.
Antioxidant activity: Marshmallow root's antioxidants help protect the delicate eye tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by environmental toxins, further reducing the risk of itchy eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and calms irritated eyes, providing relief from itchiness.
Flowers: Marshmallow flowers contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy eyes, promoting relaxation and comfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality althaea officinalis leaves and flowers from a trusted supplier or grow your own.
Dry the plant material in a warm dark place for several days to remove excess moisture.
Grind 10-15 grams of dried althaea officinalis into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powder with 250 milliliters of boiling water and let it steep for 10-15 minutes in a glass jar.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage decoctions helps with itchy eyes because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herbal extract contains borage oil, which is rich in gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), a powerful natural anti-inflammatory agent that reduces swelling and redness. Additionally, the decoction's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals, protecting the delicate eye tissues from damage and promoting healthy tear production. This natural remedy provides long-lasting relief from itchy eyes caused by allergies, dryness, or other environmental factors.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Borage decoction reduces inflammation and swelling in the eyes, which can alleviate itchiness and discomfort associated with conditions such as conjunctivitis.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in borage decoction help to protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to itchy eyes and other eye problems.

Antihistamine properties: Borage decoction contains compounds that have a natural antihistamine effect, helping to alleviate itching, redness, and tearing associated with allergic reactions or irritant exposure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eye tissues, reducing itchiness and discomfort.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have natural antihistamine properties, which can alleviate itching and redness associated with itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh borago officinalis leaves and flowers for optimal potency and quality.
Chop 10-15 grams of dried or 20-25 grams of fresh herbs into small pieces for efficient infusion.
Combine the chopped herbs with one liter of boiling water and let steep for 15-20 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding solids after 5-7 minutes of cooling time.
Store the decoction in an amber-colored glass bottle refrigerated at 2-8°c for up to 3 days or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey decoctions helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herb's ability to reduce swelling and discomfort makes it an effective natural remedy for alleviating eye irritation caused by dryness, allergies, or other factors. Comfrey's flavonoids also have a potent antioxidant effect, which can help protect the delicate tissues around the eyes from further damage, promoting faster healing and reducing itching and redness.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: Allantoin helps to soothe and calm irritated skin, including itchy eyes, by reducing inflammation and promoting tissue regeneration.
Saponins: Saponins have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce bacterial or fungal infections that may be contributing to itchy eyes and prevent further irritation.
Glucosides: Glucosides in comfrey decoctions have been shown to have analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which can help to alleviate discomfort and itching associated with dry, irritated eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in allantoin, a natural anti-inflammatory that soothes irritated eyes and reduces redness.
Leaves: Containing rosmarinic acid, which has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties to calm itchiness and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of symphytum officinale plant measuring 20-30 grams.
Rinse the harvested leaves thoroughly under cold running water for 10 minutes.
Combine the rinsed leaves with 250 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan and bring to boiling point.
Reduce heat to simmer and let it steep for 15-20 minutes or until the liquid is reduced by half.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower decoctions helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eyes. The decoction's antihistamine effects also help to reduce redness and itching caused by allergens or environmental irritants. Additionally, the decoction's antimicrobial properties can help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to itchy eyes. remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Kansas Coneflower's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can alleviate itchiness caused by oxidative stress.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as quercetin and luteolin, reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from itchy and inflamed conjunctiva.

Antimicrobial properties: Kansas Coneflower's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial, viral, and fungal infections that can cause itchy eyes, promoting healthy eye function and reducing discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Kansas coneflower plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and swelling in itchy eyes.
Seeds: The seeds of the Kansas coneflower plant have been traditionally used to soothe dry, itchy eyes due to their moisturizing properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried echinacea angustifolia root per eight ounces of water.
Combine the ingredients in a saucepan and bring to a boil for one minute.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes to allow the active compounds to extract.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Store the cooled decoction in the refrigerator for up to three days before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal decoctions helps with itchy eyes because its active compound, berberine, has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling in the eye area. Additionally, goldenseal's antibacterial and antiviral agents help to combat infections that can cause itchiness and irritation. By soothing and calming the affected tissues, goldenseal decoctions provide relief from itchy eyes, allowing for clear vision and comfort. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to treat various eye issues, making it a trusted and effective solution for itchy eye woes.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help to reduce itching, redness, and swelling in the eyes.
Beta-carotene: Converts to vitamin A in the body, which is important for maintaining healthy mucous membranes, including those in the eyes, and can help alleviate itchy eye symptoms.
Luteolin: Has anti-inflammatory and antiallergic properties, which help to reduce itching, redness, and tearing caused by allergies or irritants in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of goldenseal contain berberine, a natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm itchy eyes.
Barks: Goldenseal barks have been traditionally used to treat eye irritations and infections due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in reducing itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried hydrastis canadensis root bark and ensure it passes visual inspection for quality.
Measure 10-15 grams of dried root bark and place it into a clean glass container.
Pour boiling water over the dried herb, allowing it to steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the decoction using cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discarding solids.
Allow the liquid to cool before transferring it to dark glass containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Agrimony decoctions helps with itchy eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's bioactive compounds, such as agrimonic acid and flavonoids, work to reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from irritation. Additionally, agrimony has natural astringent properties that help to constrict blood vessels, further reducing inflammation and itching. As a result, agrimony decoctions have been traditionally used to soothe itchy, irritated eyes and provide long-lasting comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Agrimony decoctions contain flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling in itchy eyes.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds present in agrimony decoctions neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative stress, which can contribute to itchiness and irritation in the eyes.
Soothing and Calming: Agrimony's anti-irritant properties help to calm and soothe itchy eyes, reducing discomfort and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the agrimony plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with itchy eyes.
Barks: The barks of the agrimony plant contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help soothe and calm irritated eyes, providing relief from itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 tablespoons of dried agrimonia eupatoria herb and add to a large glass jar.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the herb and let steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container.
Discard the solids and store the decoction in an amber glass bottle in the refrigerator.
Use the prepared decoction as needed by pouring 1-2 tablespoons into a small bowl and gently washing your itchy eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel decoctions helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and relieve itching. The decoction's antiseptic qualities also help to soothe irritated eye tissues, calming redness and discomfort. Additionally, fennel's natural demulcent properties create a protective barrier on the surface of the eyes, shielding them from further irritation and promoting healing. Antioxidant properties: Fennel's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to itchy eyes.
Expectorant properties: Fennel's expectorant properties help thin and clear mucus from the eyes, reducing congestion and itchiness associated with allergic reactions or respiratory infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, the roots are used to reduce redness and swelling in itchy eyes.
Seeds: The seeds contain antioxidants that help soothe and calm irritated eyes, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2-3 teaspoons of dried foeniculum vulgare seeds and place them in a clean glass container.
Boil 1 liter of water in a pot for 10 minutes before reducing the heat to a simmer.
Add the measured foeniculum vulgare seeds to the boiling water and let it steep for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Store the prepared foeniculum vulgare decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein decoctions helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which work to soothe and calm irritated eye tissues. The plant's flavonoids and saponins help to reduce swelling and itching, while also combating underlying allergies and irritants that cause discomfort. Antioxidant: Mullein's antioxidant properties neutralize free radicals and protect the eyes from oxidative stress, which can contribute to itchiness and dryness.

Soften mucous membranes: The demulcent properties of mullein help soften and soothe irritated mucous membranes in the eyes, reducing itching and discomfort associated with allergies, conjunctivitis, or other conditions.

Leaves: Mullein leaves contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce redness, swelling, and itchiness in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbascum thapsus leaves and flowers in the early morning, ensuring they are free from pests and diseases.
Rinse the collected plant material with cold water, then chop it into small pieces to release its essential oils.
Combine 20g of chopped verbascum thapsus with 1 liter of boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Allow the mixture to simmer for 15-20 minutes, straining the decoction every 5 minutes to collect the liquid and discard the solids.
Store the final decoction in an amber-colored glass bottle at room temperature for up to 3 days, using it as needed by instilling a few drops into each affected eye.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass decoctions helps with itchy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The citral and geraniol present in lemongrass have a soothing effect on irritated eyes, reducing redness and swelling. When brewed into a decoction, the essential oils are released, providing instant relief from itching and burning sensations. Additionally, lemongrass has antimicrobial properties that help combat eye infections, further alleviating discomfort and promoting healthy vision.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass contains antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, alleviating itchiness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass has anti-inflammatory compounds like citral and geraniol, which reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort.
Antimicrobial properties: Lemongrass has antimicrobial agents that help combat bacterial, viral, or fungal infections causing itchy eyes, promoting a healthy eye environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lemongrass roots can help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Buds: Containing bioactive compounds like citral and geraniol, lemongrass buds have natural antiseptic and antimicrobial properties that can aid in relieving itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lemongrass leaves from a trusted source measuring approximately two cups per serving.
Rinse the harvested lemongrass thoroughly under cold running water removing any dirt or debris.
Chop the cleaned lemongrass into small pieces and combine with one quart of boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 15 minutes, then strain through a fine-mesh sieve into another container discarding the solids.
Store the resulting decoction in an amber-colored glass bottle refrigerated at a temperature below 40 degrees fahrenheit within two hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pokeweed decoctions helps with itchy eyes because its anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated conjunctiva, reducing itchiness and discomfort. The herbal remedy also contains antioxidants that help protect the eyes from further irritation and damage caused by environmental factors or allergens. Additionally, pokeweed's natural astringent qualities help to dry up excess moisture and reduce swelling, providing long-lasting relief for itchy eyes. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pokeweed decoctions may help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, which can contribute to itchiness and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pokeweed decoctions could help neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues, reducing oxidative stress and potential irritation leading to itchy eyes.

Antihistamine-like properties: Pokeweed contains compounds that may mimic the effects of antihistamines, which can help alleviate itching, redness, and other symptoms associated with allergic reactions or irritations in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe itchy eyes and reduce redness.
Barks: The barks contain tannins, which have astringent properties that can help reduce itchiness and inflammation in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh phytolacca americana leaves and flowers according to local regulations and sustainable practices.
Combine 10-15 grams of dried phytolacca americana aerial parts with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes at room temperature before straining it through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle.
Discard the solids and use the resulting liquid within 24 hours, refrigerating it if necessary to maintain potency.
Administer one teaspoon of the decoction three times daily as needed to alleviate itchiness and discomfort in the eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Porter's lovage decoctions helps with itchy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb, also known as Levisticum officinale, has been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing itchiness and discomfort. When made into a warm decoction, the flavonoids and saponins present in lovage work to reduce inflammation and ease congestion, providing fast relief from itchy eyes caused by allergies, dryness, or other irritants. This natural remedy can be used as a standalone treatment or in combination with other eye care products for optimal results.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lovage decoctions have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from itchy eyes caused by inflammation or irritation.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds present in lovage decoctions can neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress in the eyes, reducing the severity of itchiness and discomfort.

Expectorant: Lovage's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear out mucus and debris from the eyes, relieving congestion and itching caused by allergies, sinusitis, or other respiratory issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Barks: Containing compounds like berberine, which has antimicrobial and antihistamine effects, making them effective in treating itchy eyes caused by infections or allergies.
remedy_recipe: Gather ligusticum porteri roots dried or fresh in equal parts measuring about two to three tablespoons.
Boil one quart of distilled water in a stainless steel pot for approximately seven minutes.
Add the ligusticum porteri roots to the boiling water and reduce heat to a simmer for twenty to thirty minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer into a clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Cool the herbal tea to room temperature before storing it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to seven days.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat itchy eyes with herbal decoctions is to brew a strong tea from calendula, chamomile, or licorice root. Strain the mixture and cool it before applying it to the affected area using a cotton ball or pad. Repeat the process several times a day for optimal relief. This natural remedy soothes and calms irritated eyes, reducing itchiness and discomfort.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy eyes that are treated with herbal decoctions are conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and dry eye syndrome. Decoctions made from herbs such as licorice root, chamomile, and calendula help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing inflammation and itching. These herbal remedies also promote healthy tear production, alleviating dryness and discomfort. They can be used topically or taken orally to treat various ocular issues.
